title: Bulbophyllum cornutum
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum cornutum is a species of orchid in the genus Bulbophyllum. This genus belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae, within the family Orchidaceae. Bulbophyllum is one of the largest and most diverse genera of orchids, containing over 2,000 known species. The family Orchidaceae is a widespread and highly diverse family of flowering plants, with approximately 30,000 known species. The subfamily Epidendroideae is a large and diverse group of orchids, encompassing many species found in tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Bulbophyllum cornutum, also known as the horned Bulbophyllum, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum cornutum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
